heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 47.89mm with a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Today ranked 7th for the most lightning strikes detected in one day since the recording of this data started on 11 April 2025  Since 11 April 2025, the day with the most lightning strikes is 07 September 2025 when 23 were seen.

storm.status: No stormy weather is anticipated.  The last big storm was recorded on 21 December 2025, lasting 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 23 December 2025, is ranked 7th among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 8 days so far.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 6 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (193°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the outside temperature (down 2.0°C).

The past week and month had observable variation in feels like temperature (up 4.3°C), dew point temperature (up 5.1°C), outside temperature (up 4.4°C), and cloud levels (up 108 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 99.6m lower than yesterday, 108.4m lower than the past week, 331.6m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1043m minimum and 1549m maximum, with an average of 1281.8m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 1390.2m and 950.2m.
cloud levels.summary: Clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight.

summary.summary: Inside; a mild and comfortable temperature is anticipated. Outside; cold spells are likely. Also, the air will feel dry and winds will be practically absent. Drizzle or light rain possible, clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight, and low sunlight due to cloudy or overcast skies. Stable weather patterns due to high pressure.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-4.3°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
